entries.title: Yuji And Higuruma Reframe Guilt And Violence

context.label: Yuji and Higuruma in Tokyo No. 1

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen early and middle Culling Game colony action through Tokyo No. 2 Hakari/Kashimo material.

content.fact: Yuji's encounter with Higuruma in Tokyo No. 1 links colony survival pressure to guilt, judgment, and whether violence can be redirected into rule change.
content.injection: During Yuji and Higuruma scenes, let the conflict carry moral/legal weight rather than playing as only a points fight.
constraints: Keep later Shinjuku Higuruma roles gated to later decks.
antiLore: Do not treat Higuruma as a generic colony combatant with no courtroom or judgment framing.

sourceInfo.title: Tokyo No. 1 Colony
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Point Transfer Rule Changes Survival Options

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen early and middle Culling Game colony action through Tokyo No. 2 Hakari/Kashimo material.

content.fact: The added point transfer rule changes the Culling Game from pure kill-or-lose pressure into a system where players can move points and open survival options.
content.injection: After point transfer is added, let negotiation and point movement become viable scene tools alongside combat.
constraints: Do not assume later rule changes or game-ending options from this early rule addition.
antiLore: Do not continue writing the game as if players can only survive by killing for points after this anchor.

sourceInfo.title: Tokyo No. 1 Colony
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Megumi's Reggie Fight Shows Colony Strategy

context.label: Megumi and Reggie in Tokyo No. 1

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen early and middle Culling Game colony action through Tokyo No. 2 Hakari/Kashimo material.

content.fact: Megumi's fight with Reggie shows that Culling Game survival depends on layered information, territory use, and tactical reading as much as raw power.
content.injection: During Megumi's Tokyo No. 1 scenes, emphasize tactical adaptation and colony-specific uncertainty rather than simple arena combat.
constraints: Do not reveal later Megumi vessel or Shinjuku developments from this colony fight.
antiLore: Do not write Megumi's Reggie fight as a straightforward power contest with no strategic misdirection.

sourceInfo.title: Tokyo No. 1 Colony
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen early and middle Culling Game colony action through Tokyo No. 2 Hakari/Kashimo material.

content.fact: Sendai Colony is a multi-way deadlock among powerful players, making Yuta's arrival part of an unstable balance rather than a simple one-on-one mission.
content.injection: In Sendai scenes before resolution, write Uro, Ryu, Kurourushi, and Yuta as part of a volatile multi-way pressure field.
constraints: Do not use Sendai as proof that all colonies have the same player structure or stakes.
antiLore: Do not reduce Sendai to only Yuta versus one named opponent.

sourceInfo.title: Sendai Colony
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.
